provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Mwanga District Council (Parking Fees) By-laws 2002.

In these By-laws unless the context otherwise requires: “ Bus Station ” means a fixed stopping place for Buses operated by the Council on the junction of Arusha-Dar es Salaam truck Road and Mwanga-Kikweni Regional Road. " Bust Station Licence " means a licence issued under these By-laws; " Council " means the Mwanga District Council; “ Director ” means the District Executive Director; “ Motor Omnibus ” means any public service vehicle which carries or intended to carry passengers and their personal goods and is licenced to carry more than seven passengers excluding the driver.

No owner or driver of a Motor Omnibus operating a passenger service at any place within or between the area of the District and any other place outside the District shall:— (a) embark or disembark any passenger on or from the Motor Omni­bus: or (b) load or unload or permit or cause to be loaded goods on or from the Motor Omnibus at any place within the council except at the station or at a place indicated by Traffic sign Lawfull erected or placed as a Motor Omnibus stopping, place.

(1) No Motor Omnibus shall use the bus station unless the owner there of is possession of a valid licence issued in respect of the Motor Omnibus by the Council under these By-laws entitling such Motor Omnibus to use the bus station. (2) A Licence issued under this By-laws shall be for one year expir­ing on the Thirty First Day of December. (3) The fees payable for a licence issued under these By-laws shall be as set out in the Schedule here to. (4) The Council may at any time revoke any licence issued under these By-laws where the owner of the motor Omnibus convicted of an offence against these By-laws. (5) Every Motor Omnibus in respect of which a licence issued under these By-laws is in force, shall display such licence on the near side lower comer of the windscreen there of in such manner as to be clearly visible. (6) No Motor Omnibus shall be serviced, repaired or refueled at the Bus Station . (7) No person shall within the precincts of the station: (a) hawk any goods or food stuffs; (b) cause any disturbance; (c) use offensive language; (d) damage, deface, or default any part of the station or any building sign post, bench or any other property situated thereon and belonging to the Council , or (e) deposit within the precincts of the station any refuse or papers. (8) Any person who contravenes or fails to comply with any of the provisions of these By-laws shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine of 50,000/= or imprisonment not exceeding 12 months or to both such fine and imprisonment. Schedule Fees for use of the bus station 1. Motor omnibus licenced to carry more tan 31 passengers Shs. 1,000/= per day. 2. Motor Omnibus licenced to carry less than 30 passengers Shs 5000/= per day.
